notes: Builds on introductory programming.
Data structures should follow Computer Science II.
Database work is stronger after data structures.
Algorithm analysis assumes data structures fluency.
Operating systems should follow programming foundations.
Software engineering is best after the core programming sequence.
Capstone should come after core upper-level preparation.
Calculus II follows Calculus I.
Linear algebra is typically taken after initial college math preparation.
Business data communications builds on introductory IS concepts.
Systems analysis should follow early IS and communications coursework.
Analytics is stronger after business data communications and statistics.
General Biology II follows General Biology I.
General Chemistry II follows General Chemistry I.
Physics II assumes Physics I and calculus readiness.
Genetics should follow the introductory biology sequence.
Cell biology is stronger after genetics and chemistry.
Developmental psychology follows introductory psychology.
Research methods should follow intro psychology and statistics.
Experimental psychology should follow research methods.
Accounting II follows Accounting I.
Macroeconomics follows introductory economics.
Marketing management should follow principles of marketing.
Finance is stronger after accounting and economics.
Engineering Foundations II follows the first foundations course.
Circuit analysis depends on engineering foundations and calculus.
Digital logic usually follows early engineering foundations.
Signals and systems should follow circuits and calculus.
Electronics is stronger after circuits and the physics sequence.
